params.description: Returns workflow run triggered by the event you specify. For example, `push`, `pull_request` or `issue`. items.docs: The GitHub Repos API merge branch endpoint allows you to perform a repository merge by making a POST request to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/merges. This operation merges a specified branch (the head) into another branch (the base) within the same repository. You must provide the base branch name, head branch name or SHA, and optionally a commit message in the request body. The merge will be performed automatically if there are no conflicts, and the API returns the resulting commit object upon success.
